description: Phase 12.8 — Batch 2 Top 5 Review — Ready for Approval
selection_criteria: Direct public email available (verified on site)
Realistic reply chance (personal blogs > large orgs)
Low spam risk (no link farms, no paid link sellers)
Likely genuine interest in free widget

top_5.name: Real Estate Witch
top_5.reply_likelihood: Medium-High — editorial team at active content site
top_5.selected_because: Pure consumer real estate blog. Their audience IS homebuyers and sellers. Every article benefits from an embedded affordability calculator. Most natural fit of all 15 candidates.
top_5.subject_line: Free mortgage affordability calculator for your readers
top_5.email_body:
Hi Real Estate Witch team,

I've been reading your homebuying guides and love how you break down the process for first-time buyers. Your article on how much home you can afford is exactly the kind of content where our widget would help.

We built a free mortgage affordability calculator at QFINHUB.com that's embeddable on any site. It uses standard DTI ratios (28/36), factors in taxes and insurance, and gives readers an instant answer — no signup, no data collection.

Your readers could calculate their exact affordability right within your articles, without leaving the page. Here's the widget landing page:
https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

If it's useful, here's the embed code:
<iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%" loading="lazy" title="Mortgage Affordability Calculator"></iframe>

No catch — it's free, no user tracking, no ads. Just a genuinely useful tool for homebuyers.

Would this be something worth adding to your toolkit?

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
QFINHUB.com
top_5.follow_up_draft:
Hi team,

Just following up on my email from last week about the free mortgage affordability widget. Totally understand if you're busy — just wanted to make sure it didn't get buried.

The widget is live, free, and takes about 2 minutes to embed. Here's a direct link to see it in action:
https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

No pressure — just thought your readers would find it genuinely useful alongside your affordability guides.

Best,
Qasem

top_5.name: Springboard (Credit.org)
top_5.reply_likelihood: Medium — non-profit, likely receptive to free tools
top_5.selected_because: Non-profit with active housing counseling programs. Highest relevance score (90). Mortgage affordability calculator directly supports their HUD-approved housing counseling mission. Low spam risk.
top_5.subject_line: Free mortgage affordability tool for your housing counselors
top_5.email_body:
Hello Springboard team,

I'm reaching out because your housing counseling program caught my attention. The work you do helping families navigate mortgage decisions is exactly the kind of mission our free tools are designed to support.

We built a mortgage affordability calculator at QFINHUB.com that's completely free and embeddable. It uses standard DTI ratios, factors in taxes, insurance, and monthly obligations, and requires no signup or user data collection.

I think your housing counselors would find it useful — clients can explore different scenarios (income, debt, down payment) and get an instant affordability estimate. It's embeddable on any resource page or counselor portal.

Widget landing page: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed
Embed code: <iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%" loading="lazy" title="Mortgage Affordability Calculator"></iframe>

As a non-profit ourselves in spirit (everything we build is free), I wanted to offer this as a genuine resource — no strings attached.

Would this be helpful for your team?

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
QFINHUB.com
top_5.follow_up_draft:
Hello,

Following up on my email about the free mortgage affordability calculator. I know non-profits get a lot of pitches — this one is genuinely free, built to help housing counselors and their clients.

Quick demo: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

If someone on your housing team would find it useful, I'd be happy to help with any embed questions.

Best,
Qasem

top_5.name: Physician on FIRE
top_5.reply_likelihood: Medium — solo blogger, direct email, may respond personally
top_5.selected_because: Highest authority score among accessible targets (85). Large FIRE audience making big housing decisions. Has mortgage-specific content. Blog owner is directly reachable — rare among major PF blogs.
top_5.subject_line: Free calculator for your readers' biggest expense: housing
top_5.email_body:
Hi Leif (PoF),

Long-time reader here — your posts on real estate investing and physician mortgages have been really insightful.

I run QFINHUB.com (free financial calculators) and we just built a mortgage affordability widget that I think your readers would genuinely use. It calculates exactly what someone can afford using standard DTI ratios, factoring in income, monthly debts, taxes, and insurance.

Your audience of physicians and high-income professionals are making some of the biggest housing decisions out there. A quick affordability check embedded in your mortgage or housing posts would give them instant clarity.

Here's the widget: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed
Embed code: <iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%" loading="lazy" title="Mortgage Affordability Calculator"></iframe>

Completely free, no signup, no user data collected. Just a practical tool.

Would this be worth adding to your housing resources section?

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
QFINHUB.com
top_5.follow_up_draft:
Hi Leif,

Quick follow-up on the mortgage affordability widget I mentioned last week. I know you get a lot of emails — just wanted to make sure this one didn't slip through.

It's a genuinely free tool — demo here: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

No worries if it's not a fit. Thanks for all the great content.

Best,
Qasem

top_5.name: Greenpath Financial Wellness
top_5.reply_likelihood: Medium-Low — mediarelations@ may forward, but may also ignore unsolicited tools
top_5.selected_because: National non-profit with HUD-approved housing counseling. High relevance (90) and authority (80). Caveat: media relations email may not be the right channel — but worth attempting as it may forward internally.
top_5.email_caveat: mediarelations@ is media/PR contact. Email may need forwarding to housing/education team. If no response, try finding a direct contact for their housing counseling department.
top_5.subject_line: Free tool for your homebuyer education program
top_5.email_body:
Hello Greenpath team,

I wanted to reach out because your homebuyer education and housing counseling work aligns with a free tool we've built.

We created a mortgage affordability calculator at QFINHUB.com — completely free, embeddable, no signup required, no user data collected. It uses standard DTI ratios and factors in taxes, insurance, and monthly debts to give homebuyers an instant affordability estimate.

I could see this being useful for your housing counselors and their clients during homebuyer education sessions. Clients can explore different scenarios on their own time, and counselors can use it as a quick reference.

Widget landing page: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed
Embed code: <iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%" loading="lazy" title="Mortgage Affordability Calculator"></iframe>

This is a genuine offer — no commercial angle, no tracking, no ads. We build free financial tools as a public resource.

If there's someone on your housing education team this would be more relevant for, I'd appreciate a forward.

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
QFINHUB.com
top_5.follow_up_draft:
Hello,

Following up on the free mortgage affordability widget I shared last week. I understand this may not have reached the right person via mediarelations@.

Quick demo: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

If the housing education team would find it useful, I'm happy to connect directly. Otherwise, no worries at all.

Best,
Qasem

top_5.name: Young and the Invested
top_5.reply_likelihood: High — personal blog, owner directly reachable, smaller site = more personal response
top_5.selected_because: Highest reply likelihood of all candidates. Personal finance blog for young professionals considering first homes. Owner is directly reachable (riley@). Smaller, more personal sites often have the highest response rates for genuine outreach.
top_5.subject_line: Free mortgage calculator your readers will actually use
top_5.email_body:
Hi Riley,

I came across your site while researching personal finance blogs that genuinely help young professionals figure out money — really enjoyed your approach.

I run QFINHUB.com and we built a free mortgage affordability calculator widget that I think would be a natural fit for your real estate and homebuying content. Your readers are at the age where they're considering first home purchases, and this lets them instantly calculate what they can actually afford.

It uses standard DTI ratios, factors in taxes and insurance — no signup, no data collection, completely free.

Here's the widget: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed
Embed code: <iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%" loading="lazy" title="Mortgage Affordability Calculator"></iframe>

Would love to know if this would be useful for your readers.

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
QFINHUB.com
top_5.follow_up_draft:
Hi Riley,

Just a quick follow-up on the mortgage affordability widget I mentioned. I know emails get lost — totally understand.

Demo: https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

No pressure at all. Appreciate the great content you put out.

Best,
Qasem

safety_rules_applied: No emails sent — all drafts are ready_for_approval only
No contact forms used — all targets have public emails
No money or link exchange offered
No aggressive scraping — publicly visible emails only
No risky automations re-enabled (Blog Agent, Scenario Gen, Indexing Engine, Sitemap Ping remain paused)
